office_does: The sheriff leads county law enforcement, emergency operations, staffing, public-safety programs, and the office budget.
race_blurb: Incumbent Joey Kriete faces Moses Lake patrol sergeant Darrik Gregg and Grant County sheriff's sergeant Josh Sainsbury.

candidates.summary: Gregg is a patrol sergeant and former chief deputy focused on accountability, partnerships, rural visibility, and tough-on-crime enforcement.
highlights: Has about 23 years in law enforcement and former chief-deputy experience [S1][S2]
Emphasizes rural visibility and a tough-on-crime posture [S2]

safety.basis: A tough-on-crime posture and increased law-enforcement visibility directly favor enforcement.

experience.basis: Long policing tenure and former chief-deputy command work provide an extensive record.

candidates.summary: Kriete is the incumbent sheriff with more than 33 years in law enforcement, focused on proactive enforcement, partnerships, staffing, transparency, and wellness.
highlights: Has more than 33 years in law enforcement and more than 20 in leadership [S1][S2]
Supports proactive property-crime enforcement and program expansion [S2]

safety.basis: Proactive property-crime enforcement is an explicit central approach.

experience.basis: Incumbency and decades of law-enforcement leadership provide a maximal proven record.

candidates.summary: Sainsbury is a sheriff's sergeant and former chief deputy who emphasizes training, morale, engagement, and treatment-backed drug enforcement.
highlights: Has more than two decades in the sheriff's office and former command experience [S1][S2]
Supports felony drug treatment with a treatment off-ramp [S2]

safety.basis: He explicitly pairs felony accountability with a treatment off-ramp, a genuinely mixed enforcement-and-services approach.

experience.basis: Long sheriff-office tenure and extensive operational and command experience provide a proven record.
